mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, which increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 payroll level at 159,105 thousand suggests post-recession recovery momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months produce negative SPX returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains elevated above 4.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows deceleration, the real interest rate tightens, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 FEDFUNDS at 5.13% with CPIAUCSL at 314.686
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in < 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate month-over-month by more than 5%, signaling construction sector confidence and forward residential investment demand, which translates to positive equity returns within 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between HOUST acceleration and 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, indicating demand destruction and recessionary pressure that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 INDPRO at 103.1389 and UNRATE at 4.1%
regime_hint: demand contraction with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates to below 0.3% month-over-month while remaining positive, indicating consumer spending momentum is fading but not yet contracting, producing a neutral-to-negative signal for S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings growth expectations compress.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in < 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between RSAFS deceleration and 60-day returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) contracts month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that increases equity risk premium and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 UNRATE at 4.1% and PAYEMS at 159,105 thousand
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling monetary policy easing that reduces discount rates on future corporate cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 FEDFUNDS at 5.13% in post-crisis recovery environment
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial shock
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between FEDFUNDS easing and 60-day returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates month-over-month by more than 0.8% while real retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously grow above 0.5%, indicating synchronized demand and supply-side expansion that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.8% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:April 2010 INDPRO at 103.1389 and RSAFS at 710,773 million dollars in recovery phase
regime_hint: synchronized demand-supply expansion in post-shock recovery
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
